name: Chaco (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the great forests of El Impenetrable and the agricultural wealth of the province
purity; the central stripe of the Argentine national flag
the rivers Parana and Paraguay that bound the province; the colours of the Argentine flag

description: A vertical triband of green, white and light blue with a yellow Sun of May centred on the white stripe.
history: Adopted by provincial law 4937 on 17 May 2007 after a public competition. The flag was designed by Mariela Beatriz Aguilar; the colours represent the Chaco landscape — the great forests of El Impenetrable in green, the rivers Parana and Paraguay in light blue, and the white-and-blue link to the Argentine nation — while the central Sun of May ties the province to the May Revolution.

features.symbolism: green for the dense forests of El Impenetrable; white for the Argentine national flag; light blue for the rivers Parana and Paraguay that border the province
shared with the national flag of Argentina; commemorates the May Revolution of 1810
